message:
✨ Platform install missions: GKE, EKS, AKS, OpenShift, Kind, K3s + 4 operators (#297)

First batch of 10 platform install missions from the automated generator.
- 6 platforms: GKE, EKS, AKS, OpenShift, Kind, K3s
- 4 operators: Prometheus, Ingress-NGINX, Velero, ArgoCD
- All include install, uninstall, upgrade, troubleshooting, and versionNotes

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "platform-aks",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)",
+    "description": "Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for organizations looking to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Create an AKS Cluster",
+        "description": "Use the Azure CLI to create a new AKS cluster. Replace `<RESOURCE_GROUP>`, `<CLUSTER_NAME>`, and `<LOCATION>` with your desired values. For version 1.31, specify the Kubernetes version explicitly.\n\n```bash\naz aks create --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME> --location <LOCATION> --kubernetes-version 1.31 --node-count 3 --enable-addons monitoring --generate-ssh-keys\n```\n\nAfter the cluster is created, connect to it using kubectl:\n\n```bash\naz aks get-credentials --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME>\n```\n\nVerify the connection:\n\n```bash\nkubectl cluster-info\nkubectl get nodes\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Post-Install Configuration",
+        "description": "Enable the Kubernetes Metrics Server for autoscaling. Apply the Metrics Server manifest:\n\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml\n```\n\nVerify that the Metrics Server is running:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get deployment metrics-server -n kube-system\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Delete the AKS Cluster",
+        "description": "To remove the AKS cluster and associated resources, use the following command:\n\n```bash\naz aks delete --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME> --yes --no-wait\n```\n\nThis command will delete the AKS cluster and all associated resources in the specified resource group."
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade AKS Cluster",
+        "description": "To upgrade your AKS cluster to a new version, first check the available versions:\n\n```bash\naz aks get-upgrades --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME>\n```\n\nThen, upgrade the cluster to the desired version (e.g., 1.31):\n\n```bash\naz aks upgrade --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME> --kubernetes-version 1.31 --yes\n```\n\nBefore upgrading, ensure you have backups of your workloads and configurations. After the upgrade, verify the cluster status:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Cluster Not Responding",
+        "description": "If your AKS cluster is not responding, check the status of the nodes:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes\n```\n\nIf nodes are in 'NotReady' state, check the events:\n\n```bash\nkubectl describe node <NODE_NAME>\n```\n\nCommon issues include insufficient resources or misconfigured network settings."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Metrics Server Not Working",
+        "description": "If the Metrics Server is not functioning, check its logs:\n\n```bash\nkubectl logs -n kube-system deployment/metrics-server\n```\n\nEnsure that the Metrics Server is properly deployed and that the API server has the correct flags set for aggregation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Failed to Connect to AKS",
+        "description": "If you cannot connect to your AKS cluster, ensure that you have the correct credentials:\n\n```bash\naz aks get-credentials --resource-group <RESOURCE_GROUP> --name <CLUSTER_NAME>\n```\n\nAlso, verify your Azure subscription and resource group settings."
+      }
+    ],
+    "versionNotes": [
+      {
+        "version": "1.31",
+        "changes": "Introduced support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.",
+        "deprecations": "Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25."

+        "deprecations": "Removed legacy authentication methods."

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "platform-argocd-operator",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install and Configure Argo CD Operator",
+    "description": "The Argo CD Operator simplifies the management of Argo CD instances on Kubernetes, enabling GitOps workflows. It is ideal for teams looking to automate their continuous delivery processes using Git as the source of truth.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install Argo CD Operator",
+        "description": "Create the necessary resources for the Argo CD Operator. First, apply the CatalogSource, OperatorGroup, and Subscription YAML files to your cluster:\n\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f catalog_source.yaml\nkubectl apply -f operator_group.yaml\nkubectl apply -f subscription.yaml\n```\n\nEnsure you are using the correct version of the operator by checking the image in the CatalogSource. For v0.12, use the image `quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify Installation",
+        "description": "Check if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly:\n\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n olm\nkubectl get subscriptions -n olm\nkubectl get catalogsources -n olm\n```\n\nYou should see the operator pod in a Running state."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Post-Install Configuration",
+        "description": "After installation, configure RBAC for Argo CD. Create a role and role binding:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1\nkind: Role\nmetadata:\n  namespace: argocd\n  name: argocd-role\nrules:\n- apiGroups: ['*']\n  resources: ['*']\n  verbs: ['get', 'list', 'watch', 'create', 'update', 'delete']\n---\napiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1\nkind: RoleBinding\nmetadata:\n  name: argocd-role-binding\n  namespace: argocd\nroleRef:\n  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io\n  kind: Role\n  name: argocd-role\nsubjects:\n- kind: ServiceAccount\n  name: default\n  namespace: argocd\n```\n\nApply the configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f rbac.yaml\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall Argo CD Operator",
+        "description": "To uninstall the Argo CD Operator, delete the Subscription, OperatorGroup, and CatalogSource:\n\n```bash\nkubectl delete subscription argocd-operator -n olm\nkubectl delete operatorgroup argocd-operator -n olm\nkubectl delete catalogsource argocd-catalog -n olm\n```\n\nAdditionally, clean up any Argo CD instances created by the operator:\n```bash\nkubectl delete argocd <argocd-instance-name> -n <namespace>\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade Argo CD Operator",
+        "description": "To upgrade the Argo CD Operator to a newer version, first ensure you have a backup of your existing Argo CD configurations. Then, update the Subscription YAML to point to the new version:\n\n1. Edit the `subscription.yaml` to change the `name` field to the new version, e.g., `argocd-operator.v0.12.0`.\n2. Apply the updated Subscription:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f subscription.yaml\n```\n3. Verify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n olm\n```\n\nIf you encounter issues, you can roll back by reverting the Subscription YAML to the previous version and reapplying it."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Operator Pod Not Starting",
+        "description": "If the Argo CD Operator pod is not starting, check the logs for errors:\n```bash\nkubectl logs <operator-pod-name> -n olm\n```\nCommon issues may include insufficient permissions or missing CRDs. Ensure that all required CRDs are installed."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Argo CD Instance Not Responding",
+        "description": "If your Argo CD instance is not responding, check the status of the Argo CD services:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc -n argocd\n```\nEnsure that the services are up and running. If not, check the logs of the Argo CD pods for any errors."

+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Prometheus Operator",
+        "description": "Use Helm to install the Prometheus Operator. Make sure to use version v0.77.0 for compatibility with Kubernetes 1.25+.\n\n```bash\nhelm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts\nhelm repo update\nhelm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.77.0\n```\n\nVerify the installation:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n default\nkubectl cluster-info\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure Prometheus",
+        "description": "Create a custom resource definition (CRD) for Prometheus. Save the following YAML to a file named `prometheus.yaml`:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1\nkind: Prometheus\nmetadata:\n  name: prometheus-example\nspec:\n  serviceAccountName: prometheus-example\n  replicas: 1\n  podMonitorSelector:\n    matchLabels:\n      app: my-app\n  resources:\n    requests:\n      cpu: 100m\n      memory: 512Mi\n```  \n\nApply the configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f prometheus.yaml\n```\n\nCheck the status:\n```bash\nkubectl get prometheus\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Uninstall the Prometheus Operator",
+        "description": "To uninstall the Prometheus Operator and remove all associated resources, run:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall prometheus\nkubectl delete prometheus prometheus-example\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade the Prometheus Operator",
+        "description": "To upgrade to a newer version (e.g., v0.77.0 to v0.76.0), first backup your existing Prometheus configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get prometheus prometheus-example -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml\n```\n\nThen, upgrade using Helm:\n```bash\nhelm upgrade prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --version 0.76.0\n```\n\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n default\nkubectl describe prometheus prometheus-example\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Prometheus pod not starting",
+        "description": "If the Prometheus pod is not starting, check the logs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs <prometheus-pod-name>\n```\n\nCommon issues include insufficient resources or misconfigured CRDs. Ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Alerts not firing",
+        "description": "If alerts are not firing, verify the Alertmanager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get alertmanager\nkubectl describe alertmanager <alertmanager-name>\n```\n\nMake sure that the alerting rules are correctly defined and that the Alertmanager is reachable."
